agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service serving predictions at sub-100ms P99 latency during checkout. The design separates the hot prediction path (a lean inference service with pre-loaded models) from the cold training path (a weekly batch pipeline that produces versioned model artifacts). A model registry with promotion/rollback semantics gives the business safe deployments. The system is sized for a 4-person team—pragmatic, not over-engineered—using managed infrastructure where possible.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Prediction Service","responsibility":"Serves real-time delivery time predictions. Loads the active model into memory on startup, accepts feature vectors over gRPC/HTTP, returns predicted minutes. Stateless horizontally-scalable pods behind a load balancer.","boundaries":"Owns only inference. No training logic, no raw data access. Reads model artifacts from the Model Registry. Receives pre-computed features from the Feature Service."},{"name":"Feature Service","responsibility":"Computes the full feature vector from raw order inputs. Enriches order details with real-time signals (current courier load from Courier Service, weather from Weather Provider). Applies the same feature transformations used during training to guarantee train-serve consistency.","boundaries":"Owns feature engineering logic and the feature transformation code (shared library with Training Pipeline). Does NOT own raw data storage or model artifacts."},{"name":"Training Pipeline","responsibility":"Weekly batch job that pulls historical delivery data, engineers features using the shared feature library, trains a new model (gradient-boosted trees via XGBoost/LightGBM), evaluates against holdout set, and publishes a candidate model artifact to the Model Registry.","boundaries":"Owns training orchestration, hyperparameter tuning, and offline evaluation. Runs in an isolated compute environment (not on serving infrastructure). Writes artifacts only—never directly updates the serving path."},{"name":"Model Registry","responsibility":"Versioned store for model artifacts and metadata (metrics, training date, dataset hash, status). Supports promotion lifecycle: candidate → active → archived. Provides rollback by re-promoting any previous version to active.","boundaries":"Owns model artifact storage and version metadata. Does not execute training or inference. Source of truth for which model version is active."},{"name":"Model Promotion Controller","responsibility":"Orchestrates the safe promotion of candidate models. Runs shadow scoring or A/B validation against the current active model. Promotes on approval (automatic if metrics pass thresholds, or manual gate). Executes rollback on degradation alerts.","boundaries":"Owns promotion policy and rollback logic. Reads metrics from Monitoring, reads/writes status in Model Registry. Does NOT own the model artifacts themselves."},{"name":"Monitoring & Alerting","responsibility":"Tracks prediction latency (P50/P95/P99), prediction distribution drift, actual-vs-predicted error rate (once delivery completes), model freshness, and feature pipeline health. Alerts on degradation.","boundaries":"Owns observability. Consumes logs/metrics from Prediction Service, Training Pipeline, and the feedback loop. Triggers rollback signal to Promotion Controller on threshold breach."},{"name":"Delivery Feedback Ingestion","responsibility":"Captures actual delivery times post-completion and writes them to the historical delivery data store, closing the feedback loop for future training cycles.","boundaries":"Owns the write path for ground-truth labels. Does not perform training or prediction."},{"name":"Historical Data Store","responsibility":"Stores labeled delivery records (features + actual delivery time). Serves as the training dataset source.","boundaries":"Append-mostly store. Read by Training Pipeline, written by Feedback Ingestion. Partitioned by date for efficient weekly training windows."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Checkout Service (external)","to":"Prediction Service","contract":"POST /predict {weight, distance, zone, time_of_day, weather_condition, courier_load} → {estimated_minutes, confidence_interval, model_version}","coupling_type":"synchronous, runtime-critical"},{"from":"Prediction Service","to":"Feature Service","contract":"In-process call or sidecar: computeFeatures(rawInput) → featureVector. Shared library preferred to eliminate network hop for latency budget.","coupling_type":"tight (shared library) or synchronous sidecar"},{"from":"Feature Service","to":"Weather Provider","contract":"GET /current?zone={zone} → {condition, temperature, precipitation_mm}. Cached with 5-min TTL to avoid latency spikes.","coupling_type":"synchronous with cache, degradable (use fallback defaults)"},{"from":"Feature Service","to":"Courier Load Service","contract":"GET /load?zone={zone} → {active_couriers, pending_deliveries, avg_load_score}. Cached with 30s TTL.","coupling_type":"synchronous with cache, degradable"},{"from":"Prediction Service","to":"Model Registry","contract":"On startup and on SIGHUP/config change: GET /models/active → model artifact (serialized binary). Pulled, not pushed.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, startup-time only"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PUT /models/{version} with artifact + metadata (metrics, dataset_hash). Sets status=candidate.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, batch"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Historical Data Store","contract":"SQL/Parquet read: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE completed_at >= (now - training_window)","coupling_type":"asynchronous, batch read"},{"from":"Model Promotion Controller","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PATCH /models/{version}/status {active|archived|rolled_back}. Triggers config reload signal to Prediction Service.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven"},{"from":"Monitoring & Alerting","to":"Model Promotion Controller","contract":"Event: model_degradation_detected {model_version, metric, threshold, actual_value} → triggers rollback evaluation","coupling_type":"event-driven, asynchronous"},{"from":"Delivery Feedback Ingestion","to":"Historical Data Store","contract":"INSERT delivery_record {order_id, features_snapshot, predicted_minutes, actual_minutes, completed_at}","coupling_type":"asynchronous, append-only"}],"data_flow":"1. Checkout Service sends order details to Prediction Service. 2. Prediction Service calls Feature Service (in-process) to enrich raw inputs into a feature vector, pulling cached weather and courier load. 3. Feature vector is scored against the in-memory model; result returned in <100ms. 4. Post-delivery, Feedback Ingestion writes actual delivery time to Historical Data Store. 5. Weekly, Training Pipeline reads historical data, trains new model, publishes candidate to Model Registry. 6. Promotion Controller validates candidate (shadow scoring or metric gates), promotes to active, signals Prediction Service to reload. 7. Monitoring watches prediction accuracy and latency; triggers rollback on degradation.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Model Registry with Promotion Lifecycle","rationale":"Decouples training from serving. Enables safe rollback by maintaining versioned artifacts with explicit status transitions (candidate → active → archived). Business requirement for rollback is directly satisfied."},{"pattern":"Shared Feature Library (train-serve consistency)","rationale":"The #1 source of ML bugs is feature skew between training and serving. A single shared library for feature transformations eliminates this class of errors entirely."},{"pattern":"Pre-loaded In-Memory Model","rationale":"100ms P99 budget is tight. Loading a serialized tree model (XGBoost/LightGBM) into memory at startup and scoring in-process eliminates serialization and network overhead. Tree models score in <5ms for this feature set."},{"pattern":"Cache-with-Fallback for External Signals","rationale":"Weather and courier load are external dependencies on the hot path. Short-TTL caching with sensible fallback defaults prevents latency spikes and partial outages from violating the P99 SLA."},{"pattern":"Shadow Scoring for Model Validation","rationale":"Before promoting a new model, run it in shadow mode alongside the active model. Compare predictions without impacting users. Catches regressions before they reach production."},{"pattern":"Feedback Loop with Delayed Labels","rationale":"Delivery time ground truth is only available after delivery completes—hours later. The feedback ingestion pipeline handles this asynchronous labeling and closes the loop for the next training cycle."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Prediction Serving","classification":"core","aggregates":["PredictionRequest (value object: input features + model version → prediction result)","ActiveModel (entity: loaded model binary + version + metadata, singleton per instance)"]},{"name":"Model Lifecycle","classification":"core","aggregates":["ModelVersion (aggregate root: version_id, artifact_uri, status, metrics, training_metadata, created_at)","PromotionPolicy (value object: metric thresholds for auto-promotion and rollback triggers)"]},{"name":"Training","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["TrainingRun (aggregate root: run_id, dataset_hash, hyperparameters, evaluation_metrics, produced_model_version)","TrainingSchedule (entity: cron expression, last_run, next_run)"]},{"name":"Delivery Data","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["DeliveryRecord (entity: order_id, feature_snapshot, predicted_time, actual_time, timestamps)"]},{"name":"External Integrations (Weather, Courier Load)","classification":"generic","aggregates":["CachedSignal (value object: zone, signal_type, value, fetched_at, ttl)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Training","downstream":"Model Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Training produces candidate models; Model Lifecycle owns promotion decisions."},{"upstream":"Model Lifecycle","downstream":"Prediction Serving","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Model Lifecycle publishes active model version; Prediction Serving conforms to artifact format."},{"upstream":"Delivery Data","downstream":"Training","relationship":"Conformist. Training reads delivery data as-is for training sets."},{"upstream":"External Integrations","downstream":"Prediction Serving","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Feature Service translates external API responses into stable internal feature representations with fallback defaults."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"In-process feature computation (shared library) instead of a separate Feature Service over the network","gains":"Eliminates a network hop on the hot path, saving 5-20ms. Simplifies deployment. Guarantees train-serve feature consistency via a single codebase.","sacrifices":"Feature logic is coupled to Prediction Service deployment. Feature changes require redeploying the prediction service.","alternatives_considered":["Separate Feature Service via gRPC — rejected due to latency overhead and operational complexity for a 4-person team","Feature Store with pre-computed features — considered but real-time signals (courier load, weather) require online computation anyway"]},{"decision":"XGBoost/LightGBM gradient-boosted trees instead of a deep learning model","gains":"Sub-5ms inference latency (well within budget). Small model size (<50MB). Interpretable feature importances. Simple to train and deploy. No GPU required.","sacrifices":"May underperform deep models on very complex non-linear interactions. Limited ability to incorporate unstructured data.","alternatives_considered":["Neural network — rejected: requires GPU serving infrastructure, harder to meet P99 latency, overkill for tabular features with 6 inputs","Linear regression — rejected: too simple, won't capture zone-time-weather interactions"]},{"decision":"Pull-based model loading (Prediction Service pulls on startup/signal) instead of push-based deployment","gains":"Prediction Service controls when it loads a new model. Zero-downtime rolling updates. Simpler failure mode—if registry is down, service keeps serving the current model.","sacrifices":"Slight delay between promotion and all instances loading the new model (rolling). Requires a signaling mechanism (config watch or SIGHUP).","alternatives_considered":["Push-based deployment (redeploy service with new model baked in) — rejected: slower rollback, heavier deployment process","Sidecar model loader — viable but adds complexity for a small team"]},{"decision":"Weekly batch retraining instead of online/continuous learning","gains":"Simple, debuggable, reproducible. Easy to validate before promotion. Matches team size and operational capacity.","sacrifices":"Model staleness of up to 7 days. Cannot adapt to sudden distribution shifts (e.g., new zone, weather event) until next training cycle.","alternatives_considered":["Daily retraining — viable evolution path if weekly proves too stale","Online learning — rejected: complex, hard to debug, risky for a 4-person team"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Model algorithm","evolution_path":"The model registry is algorithm-agnostic (stores serialized binaries). Team can swap XGBoost for LightGBM, CatBoost, or even an ONNX-exported neural net without changing serving infrastructure."},{"area":"Retraining frequency","evolution_path":"Training Pipeline runs on a schedule (cron). Changing from weekly to daily is a config change, not an architecture change. Data store already partitions by date."},{"area":"Feature set expansion","evolution_path":"Adding new features (e.g., traffic data, historical courier performance) requires updating the shared feature library and retraining. No architectural changes needed."},{"area":"Promotion policy","evolution_path":"Promotion Controller's policy is configurable: start with manual approval, evolve to automatic promotion with metric gates, then to canary/shadow scoring."},{"area":"Serving infrastructure scale","evolution_path":"Prediction Service is stateless. Horizontal scaling via autoscaler. If volume grows 10x, add instances—no redesign."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Train-serve feature consistency","reason":"The shared feature library MUST be used by both Training Pipeline and Feature Service. This coupling is intentional and protective—violating it introduces feature skew, the most dangerous class of ML bugs."},{"area":"Model artifact format","reason":"Prediction Service and Model Registry must agree on serialization format. Changing formats requires coordinated updates. This is acceptable rigidity—format changes are rare."},{"area":"P99 latency budget","reason":"The 100ms constraint is a hard system invariant. All hot-path design decisions (in-process features, in-memory model, cached external signals) exist to protect this. Relaxing it would allow simpler designs but is not negotiable."},{"area":"Feedback loop schema","reason":"The delivery record schema (features + prediction + actual time) must remain stable for training pipeline compatibility. Schema evolution must be backward-compatible."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Feature skew between training and serving despite shared library","severity":"high","mitigation":"CI pipeline runs integration tests that score the same input through both the training feature path and serving feature path, asserting identical outputs. Log feature vectors at prediction time and compare distributions against training data weekly."},{"risk":"Model degradation goes undetected between weekly retraining cycles","severity":"high","mitigation":"Monitoring tracks prediction distribution drift in real-time (no need to wait for ground truth). Alert on sudden shifts in predicted time distributions. Once actuals arrive, track MAE/RMSE and trigger rollback if error exceeds threshold."},{"risk":"100ms P99 latency violated due to external signal fetches (weather, courier load)","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Cache external signals with short TTLs (weather: 5min, courier load: 30s). On cache miss or timeout (50ms hard cutoff), use fallback defaults derived from historical averages for that zone/time. Circuit breaker on external calls."},{"risk":"Model registry becomes single point of failure","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Prediction Service caches the active model in memory and on local disk. Registry downtime prevents model updates but does not affect serving. Registry backed by durable object storage (S3/GCS) with replication."},{"risk":"Training pipeline fails silently, model goes stale","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitoring tracks model_last_trained_at metric. Alert if model age exceeds 8 days. Training pipeline emits explicit success/failure events. Failed runs do not publish candidates."},{"risk":"4-person team is stretched thin across ML and platform concerns","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use managed services aggressively: managed object storage for registry, managed orchestration (Airflow/Cloud Composer) for training, managed Kubernetes or ECS for serving. Minimize custom infrastructure. Clear ownership: 2 ML engineers own training + features, 2 platform engineers own serving + registry + monitoring."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1 (weeks 1-3): Build the Prediction Service with hardcoded/simple model, Feature Service as shared library, and basic monitoring. Validate P99 latency in staging under load. Phase 2 (weeks 4-6): Build Training Pipeline, Model Registry (can start as S3 bucket + DynamoDB table for metadata), and Feedback Ingestion. Train first real model. Phase 3 (weeks 7-8): Build Promotion Controller with manual approval gate. Add shadow scoring. Harden monitoring with drift detection and rollback alerts. Phase 4 (ongoing): Iterate on features, model algorithms, and promotion automation.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/prediction-service/ — Python (FastAPI or gRPC) service, Dockerfile, k8s manifests","libs/feature-engine/ — Shared Python package for feature transformations, used by both serving and training","pipelines/training/ — Airflow DAG or script for weekly training, evaluation, and artifact publishing","services/model-registry/ — API + storage layer for model artifacts and version metadata (or use MLflow if team prefers)","services/promotion-controller/ — Lightweight service or script that validates candidates and manages promotion lifecycle","services/feedback-ingestion/ — Consumer that writes delivery outcomes to the data store","infra/terraform/ — Infrastructure as code for data store, object storage, compute, networking","monitoring/dashboards/ — Grafana dashboards for prediction latency, model accuracy, feature drift","monitoring/alerts/ — Alert rules for P99 latency breach, model staleness, accuracy degradation"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the shared feature library as a versioned Python package installed by both prediction service and training pipeline. Pin versions to prevent drift. Use semantic versioning on model artifacts. Implement health checks that verify the model is loaded and feature dependencies are reachable. Use structured logging with request_id, model_version, and prediction_latency on every prediction for debugging and monitoring.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT put feature transformation logic in two places (serving and training separately)—this is the #1 cause of ML production bugs","Do NOT call external APIs (weather, courier load) without timeouts and fallbacks—one slow dependency will blow your P99","Do NOT deploy a new model without shadow validation or at minimum an offline holdout evaluation with statistical significance","Do NOT store model artifacts in a database—use object storage (S3/GCS) and store only metadata in the DB","Do NOT skip versioning model artifacts—without it, rollback is impossible and the business requirement is unmet","Do NOT build a custom ML platform—use existing tools (MLflow, Airflow, S3) and glue them together. A 4-person team cannot maintain custom infra"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Serving architecture and latency optimization—well-understood problem with proven patterns","Model registry and rollback design—standard ML platform pattern","Feature consistency approach—shared library is industry best practice","Team sizing and phased rollout—realistic for 4 engineers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact model algorithm performance—need experimentation to confirm XGBoost is sufficient vs. more complex models","Courier load signal freshness requirements—30s cache TTL is a guess, may need tuning based on load volatility","Promotion policy thresholds—metric gates for auto-promotion need empirical tuning after initial deployments"],"assumptions":["Prediction volume is moderate (thousands/sec, not millions)—if higher, may need model compilation (ONNX, TorchScript) or edge caching","Weather and courier load APIs exist and are reasonably reliable","Historical delivery data is clean enough for training without major data engineering effort","Team has Python ML experience (XGBoost, scikit-learn ecosystem)"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service serving predictions at sub-100ms P99 latency during checkout. The design separates the hot prediction path (a lean inference service with pre-loaded models) from the cold training path (a weekly batch pipeline that produces versioned model artifacts). A model registry with promotion/rollback semantics gives the business safe deployments. The system is sized for a 4-person team—pragmatic, not over-engineered—us

content.thinking: Let me design a comprehensive ML pipeline architecture for this delivery time estimation system.

If volume grows 10x, add instances—no redesign."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Train-serve feature consistency","reason":"The shared feature library MUST be used by both Training Pipeline and Feature Service. This coupling is intentional and protective—violating it introduces feature skew, the most dangerous class of ML bugs."},{"area":"Model artifact format","reason":"Prediction Service and Model Registry must agree on serialization format. Changing formats requires coordinated updates. This is acceptable rigidity—format changes are rare."},{"area":"P99 latency budget","reason":"The 100ms constraint is a hard system invariant. All hot-path design decisions (in-process features, in-memory model, cached external signals) exist to protect this. Relaxing it would allow simpler designs but is not negotiable."},{"area":"Feedback loop schema","reason":"The delivery record schema (features + prediction + actual time) must remain stable for training pipeline compatibility. Schema evolution must be backward-compatible."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Feature skew between training and serving despite shared library","severity":"high","mitigation":"CI pipeline runs integration tests that score the same input through both the training feature path and serving feature path, asserting identical outputs. Log feature vectors at prediction time and compare distributions against training data weekly."},{"risk":"Model degradation goes undetected between weekly retraining cycles","severity":"high","mitigation":"Monitoring tracks prediction distribution drift in real-time (no need to wait for ground truth). Alert on sudden shifts in predicted time distributions. Once actuals arrive, track MAE/RMSE and trigger rollback if error exceeds threshold."},{"risk":"100ms P99 latency violated due to external signal fetches (weather, courier load)","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Cache external signals with short TTLs (weather: 5min, courier load: 30s). On cache miss or timeout (50ms hard cutoff), use fallback defaults derived from historical averages for that zone/time. Circuit breaker on external calls."},{"risk":"Model registry becomes single point of failure","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Prediction Service caches the active model in memory and on local disk. Registry downtime prevents model updates but does not affect serving. Registry backed by durable object storage (S3/GCS) with replication."},{"risk":"Training pipeline fails silently, model goes stale","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitoring tracks model_last_trained_at metric. Alert if model age exceeds 8 days. Training pipeline emits explicit success/failure events. Failed runs do not publish candidates."},{"risk":"4-person team is stretched thin across ML and platform concerns","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use managed services aggressively: managed object storage for registry, managed orchestration (Airflow/Cloud Composer) for training, managed Kubernetes or ECS for serving. Minimize custom infrastructure. Clear ownership: 2 ML engineers own training + features, 2 platform engineers own serving + registry + monitoring."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1 (weeks 1-3): Build the Prediction Service with hardcoded/simple model, Feature Service as shared library, and basic monitoring. Validate P99 latency in staging under load. Phase 2 (weeks 4-6): Build Training Pipeline, Model Registry (can start as S3 bucket + DynamoDB table for metadata), and Feedback Ingestion. Train first real model. Phase 3 (weeks 7-8): Build Promotion Controller with manual approval gate. Add shadow scoring. Harden monitoring with drift detection and rollback alerts. Phase 4 (ongoing): Iterate on features, model algorithms, and promotion automation.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/prediction-service/ — Python (FastAPI or gRPC) service, Dockerfile, k8s manifests","libs/feature-engine/ — Shared Python package for feature transformations, used by both serving and training","pipelines/training/ — Airflow DAG or script for weekly training, evaluation, and artifact publishing","services/model-registry/ — API + storage layer for model artifacts and version metadata (or use MLflow if team prefers)","services/promotion-controller/ — Lightweight service or script that validates candidates and manages promotion lifecycle","services/feedback-ingestion/ — Consumer that writes delivery outcomes to the data store","infra/terraform/ — Infrastructure as code for data store, object storage, compute, networking","monitoring/dashboards/ — Grafana dashboards for prediction latency, model accuracy, feature drift","monitoring/alerts/ — Alert rules for P99 latency breach, model staleness, accuracy degradation"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the shared feature library as a versioned Python package installed by both prediction service and training pipeline. Pin versions to prevent drift. Use semantic versioning on model artifacts. Implement health checks that verify the model is loaded and feature dependencies are reachable. Use structured logging with request_id, model_version, and prediction_latency on every prediction for debugging and monitoring.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT put feature transformation logic in two places (serving and training separately)—this is the #1 cause of ML production bugs","Do NOT call external APIs (weather, courier load) without timeouts and fallbacks—one slow dependency will blow your P99","Do NOT deploy a new model without shadow validation or at minimum an offline holdout evaluation with statistical significance","Do NOT store model artifacts in a database—use object storage (S3/GCS) and store only metadata in the DB","Do NOT skip versioning model artifacts—without it, rollback is impossible and the business requirement is unmet","Do NOT build a custom ML platform—use existing tools (MLflow, Airflow, S3) and glue them together. A 4-person team cannot maintain custom infra"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Serving architecture and latency optimization—well-understood problem with proven patterns","Model registry and rollback design—standard ML platform pattern","Feature consistency approach—shared library is industry best practice","Team sizing and phased rollout—realistic for 4 engineers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact model algorithm performance—need experimentation to confirm XGBoost is sufficient vs. more complex models","Courier load signal freshness requirements—30s cache TTL is a guess, may need tuning based on load volatility","Promotion policy thresholds—metric gates for auto-promotion need empirical tuning after initial deployments"],"assumptions":["Prediction volume is moderate (thousands/sec, not millions)—if higher, may need model compilation (ONNX, TorchScript) or edge caching","Weather and courier load APIs exist and are reasonably reliable","Historical delivery data is clean enough for training without major data engineering effort","Team has Python ML experience (XGBoost, scikit-learn ecosystem)"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service serving predictions at sub-100ms P99 latency during checkout. The design separates the hot prediction path (a lean inference service with pre-loaded models) from the cold training path (a weekly batch pipeline that produces versioned model artifacts). A model registry with promotion/rollback semantics gives the business safe deployments. The system is sized for a 4-person team—pragmatic, not over-engineered—using managed infrastructure where possible.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Prediction Service","responsibility":"Serves real-time delivery time predictions. Loads the active model into memory on startup, accepts feature vectors over gRPC/HTTP, returns predicted minutes. Stateless horizontally-scalable pods behind a load balancer.","boundaries":"Owns only inference. No training logic, no raw data access. Reads model artifacts from the Model Registry. Receives pre-computed features from the Feature Service."},{"name":"Feature Service","responsibility":"Computes the full feature vector from raw order inputs. Enriches order details with real-time signals (current courier load from Courier Service, weather from Weather Provider). Applies the same feature transformations used during training to guarantee train-serve consistency.","boundaries":"Owns feature engineering logic and the feature transformation code (shared library with Training Pipeline). Does NOT own raw data storage or model artifacts."},{"name":"Training Pipeline","responsibility":"Weekly batch job that pulls historical delivery data, engineers features using the shared feature library, trains a new model (gradient-boosted trees via XGBoost/LightGBM), evaluates against holdout set, and publishes a candidate model artifact to the Model Registry.","boundaries":"Owns training orchestration, hyperparameter tuning, and offline evaluation. Runs in an isolated compute environment (not on serving infrastructure). Writes artifacts only—never directly updates the serving path."},{"name":"Model Registry","responsibility":"Versioned store for model artifacts and metadata (metrics, training date, dataset hash, status). Supports promotion lifecycle: candidate → active → archived. Provides rollback by re-promoting any previous version to active.","boundaries":"Owns model artifact storage and version metadata. Does not execute training or inference. Source of truth for which model version is active."},{"name":"Model Promotion Controller","responsibility":"Orchestrates the safe promotion of candidate models. Runs shadow scoring or A/B validation against the current active model. Promotes on approval (automatic if metrics pass thresholds, or manual gate). Executes rollback on degradation alerts.","boundaries":"Owns promotion policy and rollback logic. Reads metrics from Monitoring, reads/writes status in Model Registry. Does NOT own the model artifacts themselves."},{"name":"Monitoring & Alerting","responsibility":"Tracks prediction latency (P50/P95/P99), prediction distribution drift, actual-vs-predicted error rate (once delivery completes), model freshness, and feature pipeline health. Alerts on degradation.","boundaries":"Owns observability. Consumes logs/metrics from Prediction Service, Training Pipeline, and the feedback loop. Triggers rollback signal to Promotion Controller on threshold breach."},{"name":"Delivery Feedback Ingestion","responsibility":"Captures actual delivery times post-completion and writes them to the historical delivery data store, closing the feedback loop for future training cycles.","boundaries":"Owns the write path for ground-truth labels. Does not perform training or prediction."},{"name":"Historical Data Store","responsibility":"Stores labeled delivery records (features + actual delivery time). Serves as the training dataset source.","boundaries":"Append-mostly store. Read by Training Pipeline, written by Feedback Ingestion. Partitioned by date for efficient weekly training windows."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Checkout Service (external)","to":"Prediction Service","contract":"POST /predict {weight, distance, zone, time_of_day, weather_condition, courier_load} → {estimated_minutes, confidence_interval, model_version}","coupling_type":"synchronous, runtime-critical"},{"from":"Prediction Service","to":"Feature Service","contract":"In-process call or sidecar: computeFeatures(rawInput) → featureVector. Shared library preferred to eliminate network hop for latency budget.","coupling_type":"tight (shared library) or synchronous sidecar"},{"from":"Feature Service","to":"Weather Provider","contract":"GET /current?zone={zone} → {condition, temperature, precipitation_mm}. Cached with 5-min TTL to avoid latency spikes.","coupling_type":"synchronous with cache, degradable (use fallback defaults)"},{"from":"Feature Service","to":"Courier Load Service","contract":"GET /load?zone={zone} → {active_couriers, pending_deliveries, avg_load_score}. Cached with 30s TTL.","coupling_type":"synchronous with cache, degradable"},{"from":"Prediction Service","to":"Model Registry","contract":"On startup and on SIGHUP/config change: GET /models/active → model artifact (serialized binary). Pulled, not pushed.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, startup-time only"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PUT /models/{version} with artifact + metadata (metrics, dataset_hash). Sets status=candidate.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, batch"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Historical Data Store","contract":"SQL/Parquet read: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE completed_at >= (now - training_window)","coupling_type":"asynchronous, batch read"},{"from":"Model Promotion Controller","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PATCH /models/{version}/status {active|archived|rolled_back}. Triggers config reload signal to Prediction Service.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, event-driven"},{"from":"Monitoring & Alerting","to":"Model Promotion Controller","contract":"Event: model_degradation_detected {model_version, metric, threshold, actual_value} → triggers rollback evaluation","coupling_type":"event-driven, asynchronous"},{"from":"Delivery Feedback Ingestion","to":"Historical Data Store","contract":"INSERT delivery_record {order_id, features_snapshot, predicted_minutes, actual_minutes, completed_at}","coupling_type":"asynchronous, append-only"}],"data_flow":"1. Checkout Service sends order details to Prediction Service. 2. Prediction Service calls Feature Service (in-process) to enrich raw inputs into a feature vector, pulling cached weather and courier load. 3. Feature vector is scored against the in-memory model; result returned in <100ms. 4. Post-delivery, Feedback Ingestion writes actual delivery time to Historical Data Store. 5. Weekly, Training Pipeline reads historical data, trains new model, publishes candidate to Model Registry. 6. Promotion Controller validates candidate (shadow scoring or metric gates), promotes to active, signals Prediction Service to reload. 7. Monitoring watches prediction accuracy and latency; triggers rollback on degradation.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Model Registry with Promotion Lifecycle","rationale":"Decouples training from serving. Enables safe rollback by maintaining versioned artifacts with explicit status transitions (candidate → active → archived). Business requirement for rollback is directly satisfied."},{"pattern":"Shared Feature Library (train-serve consistency)","rationale":"The #1 source of ML bugs is feature skew between training and serving. A single shared library for feature transformations eliminates this class of errors entirely."},{"pattern":"Pre-loaded In-Memory Model","rationale":"100ms P99 budget is tight. Loading a serialized tree model (XGBoost/LightGBM) into memory at startup and scoring in-process eliminates serialization and network overhead. Tree models score in <5ms for this feature set."},{"pattern":"Cache-with-Fallback for External Signals","rationale":"Weather and courier load are external dependencies on the hot path. Short-TTL caching with sensible fallback defaults prevents latency spikes and partial outages from violating the P99 SLA."},{"pattern":"Shadow Scoring for Model Validation","rationale":"Before promoting a new model, run it in shadow mode alongside the active model. Compare predictions without impacting users. Catches regressions before they reach production."},{"pattern":"Feedback Loop with Delayed Labels","rationale":"Delivery time ground truth is only available after delivery completes—hours later. The feedback ingestion pipeline handles this asynchronous labeling and closes the loop for the next training cycle."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Prediction Serving","classification":"core","aggregates":["PredictionRequest (value object: input features + model version → prediction result)","ActiveModel (entity: loaded model binary + version + metadata, singleton per instance)"]},{"name":"Model Lifecycle","classification":"core","aggregates":["ModelVersion (aggregate root: version_id, artifact_uri, status, metrics, training_metadata, created_at)","PromotionPolicy (value object: metric thresholds for auto-promotion and rollback triggers)"]},{"name":"Training","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["TrainingRun (aggregate root: run_id, dataset_hash, hyperparameters, evaluation_metrics, produced_model_version)","TrainingSchedule (entity: cron expression, last_run, next_run)"]},{"name":"Delivery Data","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["DeliveryRecord (entity: order_id, feature_snapshot, predicted_time, actual_time, timestamps)"]},{"name":"External Integrations (Weather, Courier Load)","classification":"generic","aggregates":["CachedSignal (value object: zone, signal_type, value, fetched_at, ttl)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Training","downstream":"Model Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Training produces candidate models; Model Lifecycle owns promotion decisions."},{"upstream":"Model Lifecycle","downstream":"Prediction Serving","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Model Lifecycle publishes active model version; Prediction Serving conforms to artifact format."},{"upstream":"Delivery Data","downstream":"Training","relationship":"Conformist. Training reads delivery data as-is for training sets."},{"upstream":"External Integrations","downstream":"Prediction Serving","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Feature Service translates external API responses into stable internal feature representations with fallback defaults."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"In-process feature computation (shared library) instead of a separate Feature Service over the network","gains":"Eliminates a network hop on the hot path, saving 5-20ms. Simplifies deployment. Guarantees train-serve feature consistency via a single codebase.","sacrifices":"Feature logic is coupled to Prediction Service deployment. Feature changes require redeploying the prediction service.","alternatives_considered":["Separate Feature Service via gRPC — rejected due to latency overhead and operational complexity for a 4-person team","Feature Store with pre-computed features — considered but real-time signals (courier load, weather) require online computation anyway"]},{"decision":"XGBoost/LightGBM gradient-boosted trees instead of a deep learning model","gains":"Sub-5ms inference latency (well within budget). Small model size (<50MB). Interpretable feature importances. Simple to train and deploy. No GPU required.","sacrifices":"May underperform deep models on very complex non-linear interactions. Limited ability to incorporate unstructured data.","alternatives_considered":["Neural network — rejected: requires GPU serving infrastructure, harder to meet P99 latency, overkill for tabular features with 6 inputs","Linear regression — rejected: too simple, won't capture zone-time-weather interactions"]},{"decision":"Pull-based model loading (Prediction Service pulls on startup/signal) instead of push-based deployment","gains":"Prediction Service controls when it loads a new model. Zero-downtime rolling updates. Simpler failure mode—if registry is down, service keeps serving the current model.","sacrifices":"Slight delay between promotion and all instances loading the new model (rolling). Requires a signaling mechanism (config watch or SIGHUP).","alternatives_considered":["Push-based deployment (redeploy service with new model baked in) — rejected: slower rollback, heavier deployment process","Sidecar model loader — viable but adds complexity for a small team"]},{"decision":"Weekly batch retraining instead of online/continuous learning","gains":"Simple, debuggable, reproducible. Easy to validate before promotion. Matches team size and operational capacity.","sacrifices":"Model staleness of up to 7 days. Cannot adapt to sudden distribution shifts (e.g., new zone, weather event) until next training cycle.","alternatives_considered":["Daily retraining — viable evolution path if weekly proves too stale","Online learning — rejected: complex, hard to debug, risky for a 4-person team"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Model algorithm","evolution_path":"The model registry is algorithm-agnostic (stores serialized binaries). Team can swap XGBoost for LightGBM, CatBoost, or even an ONNX-exported neural net without changing serving infrastructure."},{"area":"Retraining frequency","evolution_path":"Training Pipeline runs on a schedule (cron). Changing from weekly to daily is a config change, not an architecture change. Data store already partitions by date."},{"area":"Feature set expansion","evolution_path":"Adding new features (e.g., traffic data, historical courier performance) requires updating the shared feature library and retraining. No architectural changes needed."},{"area":"Promotion policy","evolution_path":"Promotion Controller's policy is configurable: start with manual approval, evolve to automatic promotion with metric gates, then to canary/shadow scoring."},{"area":"Serving infrastructure scale","evolution_path":"Prediction Service is stateless. Horizontal scaling via autoscaler. If volume grows 10x, add instances—no redesign."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Train-serve feature consistency","reason":"The shared feature library MUST be used by both Training Pipeline and Feature Service. This coupling is intentional and protective—violating it introduces feature skew, the most dangerous class of ML bugs."},{"area":"Model artifact format","reason":"Prediction Service and Model Registry must agree on serialization format. Changing formats requires coordinated updates. This is acceptable rigidity—format changes are rare."},{"area":"P99 latency budget","reason":"The 100ms constraint is a hard system invariant. All hot-path design decisions (in-process features, in-memory model, cached external signals) exist to protect this. Relaxing it would allow simpler designs but is not negotiable."},{"area":"Feedback loop schema","reason":"The delivery record schema (features + prediction + actual time) must remain stable for training pipeline compatibility. Schema evolution must be backward-compatible."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Feature skew between training and serving despite shared library","severity":"high","mitigation":"CI pipeline runs integration tests that score the same input through both the training feature path and serving feature path, asserting identical outputs. Log feature vectors at prediction time and compare distributions against training data weekly."},{"risk":"Model degradation goes undetected between weekly retraining cycles","severity":"high","mitigation":"Monitoring tracks prediction distribution drift in real-time (no need to wait for ground truth). Alert on sudden shifts in predicted time distributions. Once actuals arrive, track MAE/RMSE and trigger rollback if error exceeds threshold."},{"risk":"100ms P99 latency violated due to external signal fetches (weather, courier load)","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Cache external signals with short TTLs (weather: 5min, courier load: 30s). On cache miss or timeout (50ms hard cutoff), use fallback defaults derived from historical averages for that zone/time. Circuit breaker on external calls."},{"risk":"Model registry becomes single point of failure","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Prediction Service caches the active model in memory and on local disk. Registry downtime prevents model updates but does not affect serving. Registry backed by durable object storage (S3/GCS) with replication."},{"risk":"Training pipeline fails silently, model goes stale","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitoring tracks model_last_trained_at metric. Alert if model age exceeds 8 days. Training pipeline emits explicit success/failure events. Failed runs do not publish candidates."},{"risk":"4-person team is stretched thin across ML and platform concerns","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use managed services aggressively: managed object storage for registry, managed orchestration (Airflow/Cloud Composer) for training, managed Kubernetes or ECS for serving. Minimize custom infrastructure. Clear ownership: 2 ML engineers own training + features, 2 platform engineers own serving + registry + monitoring."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1 (weeks 1-3): Build the Prediction Service with hardcoded/simple model, Feature Service as shared library, and basic monitoring. Validate P99 latency in staging under load. Phase 2 (weeks 4-6): Build Training Pipeline, Model Registry (can start as S3 bucket + DynamoDB table for metadata), and Feedback Ingestion. Train first real model. Phase 3 (weeks 7-8): Build Promotion Controller with manual approval gate. Add shadow scoring. Harden monitoring with drift detection and rollback alerts. Phase 4 (ongoing): Iterate on features, model algorithms, and promotion automation.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/prediction-service/ — Python (FastAPI or gRPC) service, Dockerfile, k8s manifests","libs/feature-engine/ — Shared Python package for feature transformations, used by both serving and training","pipelines/training/ — Airflow DAG or script for weekly training, evaluation, and artifact publishing","services/model-registry/ — API + storage layer for model artifacts and version metadata (or use MLflow if team prefers)","services/promotion-controller/ — Lightweight service or script that validates candidates and manages promotion lifecycle","services/feedback-ingestion/ — Consumer that writes delivery outcomes to the data store","infra/terraform/ — Infrastructure as code for data store, object storage, compute, networking","monitoring/dashboards/ — Grafana dashboards for prediction latency, model accuracy, feature drift","monitoring/alerts/ — Alert rules for P99 latency breach, model staleness, accuracy degradation"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the shared feature library as a versioned Python package installed by both prediction service and training pipeline. Pin versions to prevent drift. Use semantic versioning on model artifacts. Implement health checks that verify the model is loaded and feature dependencies are reachable. Use structured logging with request_id, model_version, and prediction_latency on every prediction for debugging and monitoring.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT put feature transformation logic in two places (serving and training separately)—this is the #1 cause of ML production bugs","Do NOT call external APIs (weather, courier load) without timeouts and fallbacks—one slow dependency will blow your P99","Do NOT deploy a new model without shadow validation or at minimum an offline holdout evaluation with statistical significance","Do NOT store model artifacts in a database—use object storage (S3/GCS) and store only metadata in the DB","Do NOT skip versioning model artifacts—without it, rollback is impossible and the business requirement is unmet","Do NOT build a custom ML platform—use existing tools (MLflow, Airflow, S3) and glue them together. A 4-person team cannot maintain custom infra"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Serving architecture and latency optimization—well-understood problem with proven patterns","Model registry and rollback design—standard ML platform pattern","Feature consistency approach—shared library is industry best practice","Team sizing and phased rollout—realistic for 4 engineers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact model algorithm performance—need experimentation to confirm XGBoost is sufficient vs. more complex models","Courier load signal freshness requirements—30s cache TTL is a guess, may need tuning based on load volatility","Promotion policy thresholds—metric gates for auto-promotion need empirical tuning after initial deployments"],"assumptions":["Prediction volume is moderate (thousands/sec, not millions)—if higher, may need model compilation (ONNX, TorchScript) or edge caching","Weather and courier load APIs exist and are reasonably reliable","Historical delivery data is clean enough for training without major data engineering effort","Team has Python ML experience (XGBoost, scikit-learn ecosystem)"]}}
